# `@pagesmith/site` Full LLM Reference

`@pagesmith/site` is the Pagesmith site toolkit.

It owns:

- the `pagesmith-site` CLI (`pagesmith` remains a compatibility alias)
- preset loading
- server-side JSX (`@pagesmith/site/jsx-runtime`)
- CSS bundles (`@pagesmith/site/css/*`)
- browser runtime helpers (`@pagesmith/site/runtime/*`)
- Vite SSG helpers (`@pagesmith/site/vite`)
- shared SSG utilities (`@pagesmith/site/ssg-utils`)

It does not replace `@pagesmith/core`.

Keep these in `@pagesmith/core`:

- collections
- schemas and loaders
- markdown rendering
- validation
- `pagesmithContent`

Typical Vite wiring:

```ts
import { pagesmithContent } from '@pagesmith/core/vite'
import { pagesmithSsg, sharedAssetsPlugin } from '@pagesmith/site/vite'
```

Typical framework-hosted app wiring:

```ts
import '@pagesmith/site/css/content'
import '@pagesmith/site/runtime/content'
```

In that shape, collections and markdown rendering stay on `@pagesmith/core`, and `@pagesmith/site` only provides the shared prose/code-block presentation layer.

Typical JSX wiring:

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "jsxImportSource": "@pagesmith/site"
  }
}
```

Preset rules:

- default preset: `@pagesmith/site/preset`
- override via `--preset`, `PAGESMITH_PRESET`, or config `preset` / `presets`
- use `pagesmith-docs` when the project wants the built-in docs workflow

Read:

- `node_modules/@pagesmith/site/README.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/site/REFERENCE.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/site/ai-guidelines/site-guidelines.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/site/ai-guidelines/usage.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/core/REFERENCE.md`
- `node_modules/@pagesmith/docs/REFERENCE.md`
